front: Work: adapted into a seven-hour, extremely slow film by Bela Tarr; the conman Irimias convinces inhabitants of a collective farm to flee to a crumbling mansion
Work: a traveling circus comes to a small town with the world's largest dead whale; allegorizes the rise of fascism
Work: Mr. Eszter tries to supplant the 'artificial' tuning system in Bach's Well-Tempered Clavier
Work: Mrs. Eszter schemes to get herself appointed head of the town committee on moral renewal
Work: adapted by Bela Tarr as the film Werckmeister Harmonies
Work: Georgy Korin finds an unknown manuscript about four adventurers and is overwhelmed by its beauty; epigraph states 'Heaven is sad'
Work: interconnected chapters on aesthetics numbered according to the Fibonacci sequence; a Kyoto heron, a Louvre guard, and the apprentice Aulista questioning Perugino
Work: the title goddess descends into a noh actor; includes a description of the eyes of a statue of the Amida Buddha
Work: 2014 collection whose title subverts this author's reputation as 'master of the apocalypse'; ends with 'I Don't Need Anything from Here'
Work: single-sentence novella about reporting on the killing of the title animal in Extremadura
Work: a game warden decides to hunt humans after a conversion experience, in a linked novella about hunting
Author: Susan Sontag called this Hungarian novelist a 'contemporary master of the apocalypse'
Author: won the 2015 Man Booker International Prize
Author: Max Neumann illustrated this author's prose piece Animalinside, about a horrible beast in a suffocatingly small space
